title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 102.
content:
There was an Old Man of the Cape,
    Who possessed a large Barbary Ape;
    Till the Ape one dark night,
    Set the house on a light,
    Which burned that Old Man of the Cape.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edward Lear delivers a powerful performance in "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 102."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
